advices: Want to learn how to play? Read the player's guide! https://guide.draftbot.com/v/en
If you are looking for tips on how to progress, go here: https://guide.draftbot.com/v/en
When you find yourself unsure of what action to take, it can be a good idea to not react to an event.
Pets can be obtained through guild rewards.
Use {command:help} for a full list of commands.
If you want to share your thoughts with the community or the developers, join the bot's Discord server: https://discord.gg/AP3Wmzb
You can contribute to the improvement of Crownicles on the bot's Discord: https://discord.gg/AP3Wmzb
The {command:classesinfo} command lets you compare the different classes you can choose from.
Use the {command:classes} command to change class.
Some objects give a reward every day. To collect it, use the {command:dailybonus} command.
If you want to equip an object, use the {command:switch} command.
To get more information about a specific command, use the {command:help} command followed by the one you want to know more about.
The {command:guilddailybonus} command allows you to receive a reward every 22 hours if you are in a guild.
Command aliases such as `glouglou` that existed in the bot have been removed with the arrival of slash commands.
Use the {command:update} command to get information on the bot's latest update.
Crownicles is also available in other languages! Use the {command:language} command to change this setting.
Crownicles is available in other languages! Use the {command:language} command to change this setting.
You can search for a player using their ranking in a number of commands to interact with them without knowing their nickname.
To invite the bot to your server, use the link available on the bot's Discord server. The link to the Discord server can be obtained using the {command:invitecrownicles} command.
Some classes give an advantage in combat and others in reports due to a higher amount of health points.
Classes have an impact on your hit points and are therefore more or less difficult to play in reports.
It is impossible to change the name of a guild.
If you don't take good care of your pets, they could play nasty tricks on you.
When a pet is sold, it loses almost all its affection.
A pet may lose affection over time if it is not regularly nourished.
Animal exchanges via the guild shelter ({command:pettransfer}) do not cause them to lose affection.
A trained pet can bring you bonuses during your adventure.
You can give your pet a nickname using the {command:petnickname} command.
You can release a pet with the {command:petfree} command.
Soups are not the most cost-effective source of food, but they can quickly increase a pet's affection.
More information on pets can be found on the page of the guide dedicated to them: https://guide.crownicles.com/notions-principale/familiers/
More information on guilds can be found on the guide page dedicated to them: https://guide.crownicles.com/v/en/notions-principale/guildes/
More information on fights is available on the dedicated page of the guide: https://guide.crownicles.com/v/en/notions-principale/combats/
More information on the classes is available on the page of the guide dedicated to them: https://guide.crownicles.com/notions-principale/classes/
More information on state alterations is available on the page of the guide dedicated to them: https://guide.crownicles.com/v/en/notions-principale/alterations-detat
It is impossible to access the store while in prison.
The {command:shop} command allows you to purchase various items.
A detailed history of what's new in each update is available here: https://history.draftbot.com/
The bot has a website! https://draftbot.com
A player's badges are displayed on their profile in the form of reactions.
Some things can only be purchased from the guild's shop, using the {command:guildshop} command.
A higher-level guild means better daily guild rewards.
It is no longer possible to change the bot's prefix following an update to Discord forcing slash commands.
A guild has a warehouse. Its contents can be displayed using the {command:guildstorage} command.

Each pet stays full for between 30 minutes and 5 hours, depending on the animal. It’s better to prioritize higher-quality food sources if your pet eats less often, unlike heavy eaters, who can easily be fed treats.
Each guild has its own shelter. The list of animals in the shelter can be accessed using the {command:guildshelter} command.
It makes more sense to feed rare pets with better food sources than common pets, which can easily be fed using treats alone.
Any ideas to improve the bot? Use the {command:idea} command.
It's a good idea to keep some money aside so you can take care of yourself.
Each week, a ranking is made to determine which player has earned the most points. To view this ranking, use the command {command:top score} and specify the `duration` option.

Money is one of the most important resources in the game, get it from reports ({command:report}), expeditions ({command:pet}), daily effect items ({command:dailybonus}) or daily guild rewards ({command:guilddailybonus}).
Running low on HP? Before buying healing in the shop, make sure it doesn't contain a more cost-effective potion!
The potions sold in the shop are often available at very attractive prices.
To get to the top of the weekly leaderboard and win a badge, be diligent in your reporting and don't be afraid to fight.
The overall ranking is available using the {command:top score} command.
The weekly ranking is available using the {command:top score} command and the `duration` option.
Is your guild level 50 or above? Then you have a 1% chance of obtaining a badge for all guild members at each daily reward.
Even if your pet is trained, it is important to continue feeding it to maintain its affection.
How long it takes to feed your pet depends on each individual pet, so see your vet to find out more about your pet.
If you want to see another player's profile, type the {command:profile} command!
By clicking on the badges on the profiles of players who have them, you can learn how they earned them.
If you want to see another player's profile, type the {command:profile} command!
The longer you wait before an event, the more points you will get. After a certain time, the number of points will cease to increase.
You don't have to belong to a guild to feed your pet.
The game takes place in real time, so events are not available right away.
The {command:guild} command displays the guild of the mentioned person if they have one.
To find out more about the different rarities of items available, simply use the {command:rarity} command.
The {command:guild} command displays the guild of the person mentioned if they have one.
You can give your guild a description using the {command:guilddescription} command.
It is possible to name a guild elder. To do so, the guild leader must use the {command:guildelder} command.
You can give your guild a description using the {command:guilddescription} command.
It is possible to appoint a guild elder. To do this, the guild leader must use the {command:guildelder} command.
The {command:sell} command lets you get rid of an item you no longer use.
Random equipment from the shop is not necessarily profitable, especially if you already have decent equipment.
Travelling merchants never sell legendary or epic equipment, except for a certain antique dealer who sells these exclusively.
Use the {command:vote} command to support the bot!
If a guild leader leaves the guild, the guild leader status is transferred to the guild elder. If there is none, the guild is dissolved.
Use the {command:badges} command to find out more about badges.
The {command:respawn} command allows you to resume the game if you have been KO'd during your adventure.
If you would like to make a donation and receive some small rewards, click here: https://draftbot.com/donate
The classes you can choose from differ according to your level.
The game doesn't end at level 100.
The bot's birthday is April 10.
Asking for reports more often is more profitable.
You can expel a bad guy from a guild using the {command:guildkick} command. It also works with good guys.
To join a guild, you must be invited by the leader or elder of a guild using the {command:guildinvite} command.
Sharing the bot is a great way to help us.
Using a ram attack can hurt you too! Make sure you can take the hit before you use it.
The fighter with the highest speed will attack first.
Using a ram attack will also hurt you! Make sure you've got enough energy to take the recoil damage before using it.
The power of your items is limited by your stats. If you have very little defense, you won't benefit from the full power of a legendary armor.
It is possible to create your own event and add it to the bot. For more information, join the bot's Discord server: https://discord.gg/AP3Wmzb
The bot is open-source, which means that anyone can read the bot's code. Here is the link: https://github.com/Crownicles-A-Discord-Adventure/Crownicles/
Would you like to help us? Ask your server administrators to add the bot.
Crownicles does not store any of your personal data.
The {command:profile} command displays your current health status.
Rarer pets will allow you to collect better rewards.
When selling a pet, the seller does not receive money but experience for their guild.
The `/ilovedraftbot` command no longer exists.
Badges are rare and difficult awards to obtain.
Your progress on Crownicles is saved even if you play on several servers.
There are 5 different tiers of classes.
A feisty pet may occasionally cause you harm during your adventure. Don't forget to take care of it.
To feed your pet, use the {command:petfeed} command.
You gain access to better pet food sources by joining a guild.
The mythical rarity is the highest rarity of an item.
You can sell the item stored in your item reserve with the {command:sell} command.
You can follow the rare failures of the bot from this site: https://status.draftbot.com/
This is just a joke that has less than 1% chance of appearing.
When your character has died, you can resume the game using the {command:respawn} command.
When you die, you do not lose the contents of your inventory. You do, however, lose 10% of your points.
It is impossible to sell a feisty pet.
You can disable private messages from the bot using the {command:notifications} command.
It isn't possible to place a feisty pet in your guild shelter.
Releasing a feisty pet will cost you money.
It is possible to disable private messages from the bot via the {command:notifications} command.
Guild rewards can contain a badge if your guild is level 50 or above.
Winning the first place in the weekly ranking allows you to earn a badge.
You can decide on the content of future updates by using this site: https://feedback.draftbot.com
You can't feed your pet if you have an alteration.
Use the {command:map} command to obtain information about your destination.
A trip between two destinations can take between 1 and 10 hours on the main continent.
During a trip, small events may occur.
Traveling merchants sell items at attractive prices. Watch out for some scammers.
If one of your friends is in prison, you can free him or her using the {command:unlock} command.
There are over 400 different types of equipment.
Having a pet that likes you in the reports can give you luck.
Pets can be obtained on your adventure or as a guild reward.
A small event is available every 9min45 and a big one in a time between 1 and 10 hours on the main continent.
Even if you don't have a guild, you can still feed your pet with treats.
Do you want the bot to speak English here? Use the {command:language} command.
The development team always needs help. If you'd like to help us write the bot's story or anything else, join the bot's Discord server: https://discord.gg/AP3Wmzb
Thank you for playing Crownicles.
You can exclude someone from a guild without mentioning them, using their ranking. If the player to be excluded is ranked 34th overall, use {command:guildkick} and specify 34 in the `rank` argument.
This text may be useful in the future: `fzbyeifea`...
The potion of the death that kills is a very rare potion.
Travelling merchants buy back their own merchandise for a ridiculous sum.
For your health, eat at least 5 bananas a day!
Any ideas to improve the bot? Use the {command:idea} command!
A high-quality version of the world map is available at: https://crownicles.com/public/ressources/maps/map_en.jpg
To check if the bot is online, you can use the {command:ping} command.
The {command:fight} command lets you fight another player.
The rarer the pet, the more interesting the rewards in your adventure.
It is not possible to give money to another player.
You can help us write the story of the bot without knowing how to code! Join the bot's Discord server to find out more! https://discord.gg/AP3Wmzb
Don't forget to feed your pet, even if it's already trained!
Each small event earns you a few points.
Don't ride in strangers' carts, even if they have candy.
Use the "end" emoji in response to a report to access the hidden exits. Perhaps a better reward awaits you!
Can't find your way around all the characters you've met? Don't panic, there's a page dedicated to them on the wiki: https://guide.crownicles.com/lore/famous-characters
The {command:missions} command displays a list of your missions.
The {command:missionsshop} command lets you spend your gems.
The King's Treasure allows you to obtain rarer-than-average equipment.
The monetary value of a gem changes every day.
Completing missions lets you earn gems.
Secondary missions don't give gems.
New side missions can be obtained during your adventure.
The higher you level up, the more secondary mission slots you unlock.
A new daily mission is available every day.
You can find a guild by posting an announcement on the bot's official Discord server.
The king's fervor grants you 1,000 points and can be purchased in the missions shop.
It is not possible to buy the King's fervor more than once a week.
You can find out exactly how much a pet likes you from the vet.
You can find out about your pet's diet from the vet.
The vet can be contacted through the missions shop.
The daily mission is the same for all players.
More difficult missions offer more rewards.
You can recruit new members for your guild via the official bot's Discord server.
There are 3 types of missions: campaign, daily and secondary.
The daily mission is the same for all players.
When selling a pet to another player, the pet loses almost all of its love points.
Reading the tips can help you make faster progress.
You can set the `force` argument of the {command:drink} command to `true` to avoid having to confirm when you drink a potion.
The mission change does not allow you to obtain the rewards of the exchanged mission.
The mission change allows you to exchange a side mission you don't like for a new one.
You can view our privacy policy by following this link: https://crownicles.com/confidentialite/
You can review our terms of use by following this link: https://crownicles.com/conditionsutilisation/
By fighting opponents, you earn glory points.
A battle season ends on Sunday morning, between 1:00 a.m. and 2:00 a.m. (French time). The game is paused for 20 minutes while waiting for the results.
Depending on your league, your profile is displayed in a different color.
You can get a bonus every Sunday based on your league using the {command:leaguebonus} command.
The number of points earned in the league bonus depends on your ranking in the glorious ranking.
A fighting season ends on Saturday evening French time.
You must have completed enough ranked fights using the {command:fight} command to be eligible for the glorious ranking.
You must have completed enough ranked fights using the {command:fight} command to receive a league bonus at the end of the season.
You can have up to 3 ranked fights per opponent per season.
In case the fight ends in a tie, the fighters' glory points are affected.
If you use an attack that costs too much, there's a small chance it will still work.

Each attack costs {emote:unitValues.breath} breath; each class has a certain amount of regeneration per turn {emote:unitValues.breathRegen}, and a starting amount of breath. Be careful with your actions, or you may not be able to defend yourself properly by the end of the battle.
Using an attack that costs less {emote:unitValues.breath} breath than your regeneration rate {emote:unitValues.breathRegen} allows you to regain breath.
During combat, you cannot accumulate more breath than the maximum allowed for each class. Once you reach that maximum, you can use more costly attacks so as not to waste a single bit of it.
Some pets, such as the elephant, the otter, or the sea lion, allow you to regain a little extra breath {emote:unitValues.breath} during combat.
The higher your league, the better the equipment you receive using the {command:leaguebonus} command.
The player with the most glory at the end of a fighting season wins a badge.
Regular battles lead to tempting rewards at the end of the week.
Watch your breath regularly to avoid losing a turn by opting for an overpriced attack.
Ranked battles consume your energy. You'll have to wait to recover some.
You can consult your glorious ranking with the {command:top glory} command.
Potions and items that advance time do not allow you to recover your energy.
Potions and time-saving items do not speed up loading times for daily rewards.
Time-saving potions and items do not speed up loading times for guild rewards.
Potions and time-saving items do not speed up loading times for end-of-season rewards.
If you lose a fight due to inactivity, all of your energy will be lost.
It's said that no one around Claire de Ville lacks the energy to fight.
In your adventure, you may find healing potions that allow you to regain life.
In your adventure, you may find energy potions that allow you to recover your energy.
If you lose a fight due to inactivity, all your energy will be depleted.
Have you noticed any new tips? That's because we added new ones! (That'd be unsettling if it was for another reason)
It is said that there are hostile, virtually unexplored islands beyond the seas.
Your guild members can help you fight monsters when you visit a mysterious island.
You can see which members of your guild are on a mysterious island by using the {command:guild} command.
The {command:guild} command lets you see the number of allies who will help you on the mysterious island.
An "ally" is a member of your guild who is currently on or has recently left a mysterious island.
Hands-on-table players stay away from the Road of Wonders, the Marshy Road, and Mount Célestrum, because it is said that a spirit lurks there and specifically preys on them.
You can only release one pet per hour.
You can't join a mysterious island if you've just joined a guild.
It is said that rare equipment becomes cheaper around the road of wonders.
Shell game players stay away from the Road of Wonders, the Marshy Road and Mount Celestrum because it is said that a spirit haunts these places and targets them specifically.
Have you found all the little secrets of the map?
Guilds can level up to level 150.
Don't hesitate to visit the vet to have your pet's age assessed.
Your pet can assist you in battle.
Pets have different ways of helping you in battle, and you can find out more about your pet at the vet's.
It is said that shell game players avoid the Road of Wonders.
It is said that shell game players avoid the Marshy Road.
It is said that shell game players avoid Mount Celestrum.
There are rumors of a dwarf living in the mountains of Mount Celestrum who possesses a surplus of gems.
The dwarves of Mount Celestrum are renowned for their love of animals.
Legendary and mythical potions are not available in today's potion.
Potions without effect are not available in today's potion.
During a battle, it is not possible to have two status alterations at the same time.
There are about 100 different pets.
It is said that the dwarf of Mount Celestrum is a great animal lover.
The spirit of the seas cannot be burned.
Paladins resist vampire attacks.
Mages cannot be petrified by a pet.
Some creatures cannot be made to bleed.
Combat potions lose one use at the start of each encounter if they are equipped. Their effect will be active for the entire duration of the combat!
Unlike classic potions, combat potions cannot be drunk with the command {command:drink}.
There are three types of combat potions: attack, defense and speed.
Combat potions lose one use with each fight started using the {command:fight} command. Some potions have multiple uses and can therefore be reused!
Equipping a speed potion can allow you to attack first in battle.
A good defense potion can make all the difference against a powerful opponent.
Combat potions can be consumed during reports if a combat is triggered.
Don't forget to equip a combat potion before issuing the command {command:fight}.
You can equip a combat potion in your inventory with the command {command:switch}.
Combat potions may be consumed when reporting if a battle is triggered.
If your combat potion still has uses after a fight, you can reuse it in your next encounter!
If you have an equipped potion and are attacked, its effect will be applied even if you didn't initiate the fight.
If you are attacked and have a combat potion equipped, it has a 90% chance of losing one use. Its effect will still be active during the fight!
A well-chosen combat potion can compensate for the weaknesses of your current class.
Rare combat potions are more powerful, but also harder to find.
From level 5 onwards, you may come across a mysterious female warrior who talks about expeditions.
Expeditions allow you to send your pet off to find treasure while you continue your adventure.
To unlock the expeditions, you must have a well-fed, non-feisty pet, be level 30, belong to a guild, and have presented your pet to Talvar.
The longer the expedition, the greater the rewards, but it will also consume more food.
The speed of your pet affects the duration of expeditions: a fast pet will return sooner.
The strength of your pet reduces the risk of failed expeditions.
Your pet's affection influences its chances of success on expeditions.
An expedition pet cannot help you in combat if you are the attacker.
Cancelling an expedition or calling your pet back loses affection, and the penalty increases if you do it several times a week.
Each expedition location offers different bonuses: mountains earn more money, forests more experience.
Far-flung expeditions take longer, but can bring greater rewards.
Your pet needs to be fed before setting off on an expedition.
An angry pet will refuse to go on an expedition.
The food consumed by the expedition comes from your guild's stock.
Three expeditions are proposed each time: two close to your position and one far away.
Three expeditions are proposed each time: two close to your position and one far away.
Tokens let you move faster right from the start of the adventure.
Tokens allow you to move faster across the map using the {command:report} command.
You can obtain tokens through pet expeditions, or by buying them in the store. You also get 3 free each day.
Tokens are unlocked from level 5.
A token allows you to jump straight to the next stage of your journey.
The longer you're busy, the higher the cost in tokens to advance.
The token merchant limits sales to 10 tokens per day and 30 per week. Manage your purchases wisely!
Expeditions to the swamps earn more points than other destinations.
The maximum number of tokens you can accumulate is 20.
The store limits token purchases to 10 per day and 30 per week. Be careful with your purchases!
If you send your pet to a place it dislikes for a short expedition, the risk of failure increases. Long expeditions are not affected.
On an expedition, danger is the primary factor leading to failure. Terrain acts as a secondary factor and can make the expedition more difficult.
Sending your pet to a place it likes increases the rewards of the expedition. Conversely, places they don't like reduce their rewards.
If you are a member of a guild, you can free the shelter's pets in addition to your own using the {command:petfree} command.
On expeditions, Danger is the main factor in failure. Terrain adds a secondary modifier and can make expeditions more difficult.
Some expedition terrains are riskier than others: plains and coasts are relatively safe, while caves and ruins present more danger.
If you're a member of a guild, you can free the shelter's pets in addition to your own with the command {command:petfree}.
During your travels, you may come across an oracle guarding an ancient altar. He'll ask you to contribute to a collective kitty: when it's full, a blessing is activated for all players!
In front of the oracle, several types of contributions are available to you, such as a fixed amount or an amount based on your level. Choose according to your means!
Blessings benefit all the world's adventurers, not just those who have contributed. Even a small offering helps the entire community!
There are nine different types of blessing: they can improve your gains in money, score, battle loot, pet affection and much more. Use {command:blessing} to see which blessing is active.
When you contribute generously to the altar of the oracle, you have a chance of receiving a bonus: gems or a mysterious object as a reward for your devotion.
The oracle does not appear while a blessing is already active. Once the blessing has expired, you can cross paths with it again.
The Amplified Rage Blessing doubles rage gains, making it easier to defeat monsters.
A blessing lasts between 8 and 24 hours. See {command:blessing} for remaining time and active effect.
If the oracle's pool isn't filled in time, it expires and resets with a more appropriate threshold. Don't wait too long before contributing!
The Amplified Rage Blessing doubles rage gains, making it easier to reach higher levels.
When the Battle Loot Blessing is active, your battle rewards (money and points) are multiplied by 4, with the exception of glory. It's the perfect time to keep on fighting!
The Pet Love Blessing doubles affection gains. Take the opportunity to strengthen your bond with your companion!
Thanks to the blessing of daily missions, your mission rewards are doubled. Remember to complete your missions while it's active!
In the kitchen, the experience gained from a failure remains the same; only the total experience decreases if the recipe level is more than three levels higher than your own.
The seeds are easy to plant in empty spots in your garden, where they will gradually sprout over the course of a few hours and grow into mature plants.
Watering the garden is an excellent way to speed up the growth of your plants by advancing their development by one hour each time you water them.
Improving your home at the notary's office can help your plants grow faster.
Every week, the herbalist offers three seeds of varying levels of difficulty so you can expand your collection.
Some seeds are provided free of charge by the gardener, whom you may meet during your travels, provided you meet certain conditions.
Once composted, each plant produces specific materials from among three possible options.
You can only plant a seed if you have both a seed in stock and an empty plot in your garden.
When your plant stock is full, you can compost some of the surplus crops to turn them into materials.
Regular purchases at the herbalist's shop provide an alternative source of income for those who have extra gold coins and want to expand their collection more quickly.
A plant that is ready for harvest can remain on hold indefinitely, allowing for great flexibility in managing your farming schedule.
Composting materials go directly into your inventory, where they can be used immediately to upgrade your equipment or in various recipes.
Your bed resets once a day and allows you to regain health points for free, based on the level of your home.
The storage chest starts out small but grows as you make improvements to your home. Each category of items has its own storage space.
Items stored in the vault do not count toward your weight limit: take advantage of this to store emergency supplies or bulk materials.
The "Swap" function in the inventory lets you quickly swap an equipped item with a stored item.
Put together a complete set of emergency supplies in your trunk so you can make strategic changes without having to go back to the store.
Each home upgrade increases storage space: investing in your home offers long-term benefits beyond just the bed.
Plant storage works separately and does not affect your weapon or armor inventory: focus on harvesting rather than hoarding seeds.
Harvested plants can be stored in the pantry without spoiling: you can keep them on hand until you have enough to cook with.
With Cœur Sylvestre, you can manage your garden remotely without having to go there in person—which is convenient when you're exploring other regions.
Growing plants provides materials for composting and food for your pets in the kitchen: it's an investment that pays off.
A well-upgraded house offers you more than just storage space: each upgrade unlocks new features, such as the kitchen or the garden.
The city's stores restock their inventory every day. Check them out regularly to find new items and the best deals.
The inn offers meals to help you regain your energy and rooms to help you recover your health. The available meals change daily.
The herb shop sells seeds for growing plants. You'll also find an exclusive selection of exotic plants that changes every week.
The lumberjack sells wood of various grades, perfect for craftspeople who want to make sturdy items. Prices vary depending on the type of wood you're looking for.
The court veterinarian treats your pets and makes them happier. The treatments are inexpensive and can significantly increase your pet's affection points.
The materials merchant offers essential resources for crafting—always handy when you're missing a crucial ingredient for your recipe. His inventory is limited but varied.
Make managing your budget a priority. Before buying an expensive item, check your finances and make sure you have enough money left over for other urgent expenses.
Meals at the inn help you regain your energy. An inexpensive meal like this will allow you to get back into battle more quickly.
Inn rooms offer excellent value for money when it comes to quickly restoring your health. The more expensive options restore more health in a single night.
Gems are a special currency that give you access to high-quality items. Save them for truly important purchases rather than spending them on a whim.
The lumberjack sells wood in bundles of various sizes. Buying a large bundle works out cheaper per unit than buying several small bundles.
When you suffer a status effect, you can cure it for a moderate fee directly from your adventure log.
The vet is very affordable compared to the benefits they provide. They can tell you a lot of details about your pet, and each visit strengthens your bond with them.
The materials merchant offers random items, mostly common ones, but may occasionally offer rare items.
Inventory expansions purchased from the Tannery increase your storage space. Investing in them early will save you a lot of trouble later on.
Garden expansions increase the number of plants you can grow at the same time. Unlock them gradually as your gardening needs grow.
Before each expedition with your pet, check to make sure you have enough food supplies. The inn or the guild shop can provide the necessary rations.
The game's various currencies serve different purposes. Money buys almost everything, tokens let you fast-forward time, and gems unlock unique purchases.
Plan your purchases based on your current goals. If you're preparing for a long expedition, buy food well in advance of your departure.
Be mindful of your daily purchase limits for certain items. Daily potions and tokens have purchase limits that you should be aware of.
Daily changes in store inventory create a dynamic economy. Take advantage of slow periods to buy what you need before prices go up.
Blacksmiths will upgrade your weapons and armor up to level 4, but this requires specific materials and an increasing amount of money.
Each upgrade level becomes more expensive: going from level 0 to 1 requires far fewer resources than going from level 3 to 4.
Materials—which can be used to make upgrades or cook various recipes—can be found in the wild as you travel the world, fight monsters, or have your pet bring them back. You can also buy them.
If you're short on materials at the blacksmith's, you can buy them directly, but they'll be expensive.
Rare equipment costs more to upgrade than common equipment: the item's rarity has a significant impact on the forging cost.
Some very rare pieces of equipment, such as legendary items, cost a fortune to upgrade to the next level.
Before undertaking a costly improvement, check your inventory to see if you already have all the necessary materials.
Disenchanting removes the magic from an enchanted item, but you have to pay the blacksmith for this delicate procedure.
The disenchantment costs are higher for powerful gear and runes, since the process requires more skill.
Once disenchanted, your equipment loses its enchantment and becomes ordinary again, but the weapon or armor itself survives the process.
The enchanter visits different cities and offers only one rune at a time, changing his selection from day to day.
Gems are rare and valuable: save them.
Each rune is specific to one type of equipment: some work on weapons, others on armor.
A weapon that is already enchanted cannot be enchanted again.
The home upgrade station also lets you upgrade your equipment, just like at the blacksmith's in town, but the upgrades will be limited by your home's level.
The equipment button lets you switch between the equipment you're currently wearing and your spare gear: this is useful for quickly testing different setups or adapting to a new situation.
Rare weapons and armor require more materials to upgrade than common ones do.
Gradually collect your materials by defeating specific monsters that drop what you're looking for.
Enchanting increases the power of equipment, but it does not replace upgrading at the blacksmith: the two systems complement each other.
Plan your upgrades and enhancements based on your budget and the materials you have available.
Attack enchantments increase the damage you deal, and Defense enchantments reduce the damage you take: their effects directly impact damage in combat, without appearing in your stats.
The "PvP Attack" enchantment only boosts your attacks against other players, "PvE Attack" only against monsters, while "Attack" works in all battles.
The Fire Aspect, Frost Aspect, and Venom Aspect enchantments amplify the damage from the Burn, Frozen, and Poisoned status effects you inflict on your opponents, respectively.

In contrast to the elements, the "Aspect of Fire" enchantment partially protects you from freezing, "Aspect of Frost" reduces the burns you sustain, and "Aspect of Venom" acclimates your body to poison.
A guild's treasury gradually fills up thanks to contributions from all its members. Every coin contributed helps the guild build and maintain its buildings.
A well-developed guild hall can easily accommodate all members' pets, and you'll still have room for a makeshift companion.
The guild shop offers pet food, paid for out of the guild's shared funds. It is a shared service that all members can use.
The menagerie works best when it's regularly renovated. A well-maintained building allows your creatures to thrive day after day.
Voluntary contributions to the treasury demonstrate your commitment to the group and help speed up improvements to the guild's buildings, which will benefit everyone.
Pet expeditions allow you to send your companion on a mission to earn rewards based on the duration, risk, difficulty, and terrain type of the area.
The guild warehouse stores common food that members can use to feed their pets.
The warehouse level increases food storage capacity and allows the guild to produce food daily.
Each city offers a variety of services accessible through a central menu: the inn, the enchanter, the blacksmith, and shops are organized into a system of intuitive submenus.
The Royal Market serves as a central hub within the castle, bringing together several valuable services: the gem trade, the kingdom’s tax records, the king’s devotion, and rare items.
Each inn offers a unique atmosphere: from the luxurious Auberge du Seigneur to the cozy Gorgée Brûlante, every establishment tells a story.
Beyond just the services they offer, exploring a city’s various inns enriches your gaming experience and reveals the local culture of each place.
Every town has its own specialty shops: the Tanner sells bags to expand your inventory, the Herbalist offers seasonal harvests, and the Lumberjack supplies fuel.
If you visit these cities regularly, you'll discover that each one is known for its own specialties: some are commercial hubs, while others are centers of manufacturing or repair.
Not all services are updated at the same time: some stores update their inventory daily, others weekly, and still others offer random surprises.
Systematically explore the shops in each city so you don't miss out on any useful services, especially since some artisans travel regularly.
The traveling enchanter Mernil offers a different rune every day, which means that visiting him regularly provides new enchantment opportunities.
Staying in town to rest, restock, and manage your inventory will help you survive longer on future adventures.

The decision to stay in town or continue the adventure depends on your plans: if you want to improve your property, tend your garden, try out different recipes, or take care of your health, it might be best to take a strategic break.
Every city has its own economy: prices for services and accommodations can vary significantly from one place to another.
The room you rent at the inn affects your health recovery: a Royal Suite restores much more health than a Horrible Room, but at a higher cost.
After visiting several cities, you'll find that some stores offer better selections or prices: it may be worth exploring the world before you make your purchases.
By paying attention to the intuitive structure of the city menus, you'll quickly learn how to access all the services and make the most of your adventure time as an experienced player.
Materials are automatically collected during your battles against monsters and creatures you encounter on expeditions. The rarer the creature, the higher the quality of the materials you obtain.
You'll find the materials you've collected in the "Materials" tab of your inventory, which is easily accessible via the dedicated command. They are stored separately from your gear and items.
To upgrade an item beyond its maximum level, you must go to the blacksmith in town with the appropriate materials and the necessary money.
If you're missing certain materials for an upgrade, the blacksmith can provide them for an additional payment in gold. The price increases gradually if you purchase multiple missing materials.
Dangerous expeditions and intense battles are your best sources of rare, high-quality materials. The more significant challenges you take on, the more abundant your rewards will be.
A material never disappears by accident. Once collected, it remains in your inventory unless you deliberately sell it or use it for an upgrade, which helps you avoid unfortunate losses.
Planning your upgrades well in advance allows you to gather the necessary materials gradually, rather than suddenly finding yourself short of them when you visit the blacksmith.
Different types of materials accumulate at different rates depending on your adventures. Some will always be in surplus, while others will remain scarce, which shapes your strategy.
Trading or selling your excess materials to the merchant is still a great way to turn your progress into cash and fund the purchase of items from merchants in town.
Upgrading equipment permanently consumes your materials: there's no going back. So choose your upgrades carefully and based on your priorities.
The inn is the perfect place to rest up and regain your strength while traveling through the countryside.
You can only eat each meal at the inn once a day, so plan your timing strategically based on your situation.
Inexpensive meals like bread and cheese provide a moderate amount of energy but are sufficient for less urgent situations. Save the more expensive dishes for critical moments.
The inn's rooms come in three levels of comfort, each restoring a different amount of life. Adapting your choice to your current needs avoids unnecessary spending.
Each inn offers a slightly different selection of meals, reflecting the local cuisine and the counter's specialties. Exploring several establishments will allow you to discover new specialties.
The Auberge du Seigneur exudes an atmosphere of luxury and elegance, attracting renowned adventurers who are happy to share their advice at the bar.
La Halte des Braves is the oldest inn in the region, filled with the festive energy of soldiers and the camaraderie of seasoned adventurers.
The Burning Sip owes its name to a distinctive spiced beer, but the inn remains warm and welcoming despite the reputation of its strong drink.
Don't underestimate the Bloody Mug: although the atmosphere is gloomy and the food is basic, the place appeals to hardened warriors who appreciate its straightforwardness.
Setting a regular budget for meals at the inn and veterinary care allows you to manage your finances without any disastrous surprises.
Unlike meals, hostel rooms have a longer cooldown period, so only book them when you really need to recover your health.
A pet that receives good care from the veterinarian becomes a more reliable and affectionate companion, capable of accompanying you on your quests and expeditions for longer.

The Royal Market is now your new hub for matters related to the kingdom's missions. The scholar who works there can offer you alternative mission contracts or valuable rewards in exchange for your accumulated gems.

The Quest Swap is a handy feature of the Royal Market: you can replace a quest you don't like with another one, chosen at random. Your first swap of the week is free, but each additional swap will cost gems.
Three types of missions await you: daily missions that offer generous rewards once a day, campaign missions that progress slowly but surely toward a major final reward, and random side missions.

Only campaign missions and the daily mission reward you with gems. Side missions you encounter along the way don't directly reward you with gems, but they do offer other valuable rewards to help you progress.
The exchange rate for gold gems at the Royal Market fluctuates daily. Checking the market analysis can help you determine whether tomorrow will be a better day to convert your gems.

Side quests appear randomly during your travels and cannot be scheduled. However, you can use the quest swap feature at the Royal Market to decline a quest that seems too difficult and replace it with a new one.
Daily quests reset once a day at a set time. Plan your gameplay so you never miss this reward window, which is your main source of gems.
When it comes to gems, invest first in permanent upgrades rather than temporary boosts. Permanent upgrades will serve you well long after the day you purchase them.
The mission swap doesn't give you a better mission in exchange, but a completely random one. If the first one seemed impossible, the second one might turn out to be even harder.
The gems earned through generous contributions to the oracle are unpredictable but valuable. Each contribution has a chance to reward you with gems or a mystery item.
Not all carriages that offer you a quick ride to a distant destination are driven by honest merchants.
The puzzles or challenges that some travelers set for you often determine whether you deserve a reward or simply have to leave.
Some events offer experience or point rewards that vary depending on your level and progress.
The witch may give you a decent potion or leave you with unexpected effects, depending on your instincts and how lucky you are at the moment.
During certain small events, Aldéric will offer you items; if you decline to buy them, they may be offered again later.
Each kitchen level brings improvements: a lower failure rate, resource savings, production bonuses, and the unlocking of secret recipes.
The prices charged by the herbalist naturally vary from day to day, making it worthwhile to compare prices from week to week.
Plant storage space is gradually purchased from the tanner at increasingly higher prices, allowing for the gradual accumulation of more varieties.
The gardener you meet along the way may give you seeds for free—as long as you fulfill one of his requests, such as traveling at night.
There are seeds reserved for players who have specific pets or certain class affinities, creating an incentive to diversify your forces.
Composting on a large scale depends on your personal strategy; some prefer the immediate results of a conversion, while others prefer a gradual buildup.
The $t(models:talisman.remoteHarvest) is a one-time purchase that allows you to harvest your mature plants without having to physically return to your home.
Your long-term gardening strategy should strike a balance between immediate consumption and patiently building up a collection of rare plants.
A residence in a city far from your main home serves as a temporary refuge: there, you have access to your safe and a bed, though the level of care provided is limited.
You may own several properties—one in each city that interests you—but only the one in the city where your primary residence is located accrues a daily income paid by the notary.
Daily guild rewards are distributed to all members based on the guild's level.

Every day, a random reward awaits your guild—health points, gold, pet food, a pet, status effect healing, personal and guild experience, or a time bonus. The type depends on luck and the guild's level.
Regular contributions to the treasury strengthen the bond between members and speed up improvements to the guild's buildings.
Certain types of daily rewards—such as group care—can only be obtained if at least one member needs them. Mutual aid is at the heart of the system.
Pet food purchased from the guild shop is paid for with guild funds. To conserve the guild's resources, it is best to reimburse the amount spent.
A well-equipped guild with a shop, a warehouse, and a menagerie can be self-sufficient in pet food without incurring additional expenses.
Joining an established guild with a well-developed territory offers immediate benefits—shelter, food, and training. It's a collective investment for everyone.
A wise treasurer plans for long-term improvements by gradually building up funds. The group's patience is what builds the greatest estates.
The longer an expedition is, the greater the potential rewards.
A hungry pet can't go on an expedition; you have to feed it first before sending it on a mission.
Making sure your pet has enough food for the entire duration of the expedition is essential to avoiding failure.
Feeding your pet regularly increases its affection, which improves its performance on expeditions.
There are several types of pet food, each with a different cost and a different effect on affection.
Cultivated plants can be turned into food for herbivorous pets, which saves money.
Treats are the cheapest food, but they don't show as much affection as a good piece of meat or some soup.
How often a pet asks for food depends on how long it stays full.
Soups, which can be made from plants, can significantly increase a pet's affection. They are useful for training a pet quickly.
Selling a pet involves a transaction with another player and must be accepted by that player.
A pet that has been sold loses almost all of its affection and acts as if it had never been trained.
Transferring a pet through the guild is a better way to distribute pets without losing the affection they've earned.
The Menagerie allows you to passively maintain and improve the affection levels of the shelter's pets.
The "Stay in Town" button lets you continue exploring the available services without having to head out to a new destination.
If you've eaten recently, the message will tell you the exact time when you can eat again.
Wait times for services (meals, sleep, enchantments) are displayed using Discord timestamps that automatically update to your time zone.
The blacksmith can disenchant items or upgrade their levels, while the enchanter adds new properties to them: their roles complement each other.
Some missions or events require you to stay in town to make progress, while others require you to continue on your way: read the objectives carefully.
The inns offer different meals each day as part of a rotating menu, which means that certain energy bonuses are only available on specific days.
Lighting or relighting your kitchen stove consumes a wood-like fuel, prioritizing the most common resources.
You can pin a recipe to save it and make it later. A recipe whose outcome is unknown cannot be pinned.
Getting your kitchen stove back up and running opens up a whole new range of recipes.
In the kitchen, you can use ingredients and plants to make various items, such as food for your pets or potions.
Upgrading your home unlocks additional cooking slots, giving you access to a wider variety of recipes.
Some recipes must first be discovered during your adventure before you can craft them.
The most difficult recipes yield the most cooking experience.
Leveling up in cooking makes you more efficient in the kitchen and unlocks various benefits.
Starting at Cooking Level 11, there is a small chance that you won't use up certain ingredients while preparing dishes.
A great royal chef rarely fails at any recipe.
If you need firewood, the lumberjack at Village Coco can sell some to you.
The kingdom's veterinarians are located in Mergagnan and Claire de Ville: feel free to bring your pet to see them.
The Mergagnan Tanner lets you expand your inventory.
Claire de Ville Stock Exchange offers a trading service.
Gems can be exchanged for unique rewards at the King's Castle.
If you're looking for materials, visit the Fortified City: a merchant there can sell them to you.
An herbalist has opened a shop in Village Coco.
